description: A green twig that bends without cracking is 'luwi'. A plan can be 'luwi' when it changes with circumstances without losing its purpose. 'thuroi' (resilient) concerns recovery after disruption rather than the ability to change shape or arrangement.
articulatory_notes: Begin with lateral 'l': touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge and let air pass along the sides of the tongue into rounded high-back 'u'. For 'w', round the lips and raise the back of the tongue, then move forward and unround into high-front 'i'. Stress falls on 'lu'.
sound_symbolism: No stop interrupts 'luwi': lateral 'l' passes into rounded 'u', then the lips and tongue shift together through 'wi'. That uninterrupted motion is the word's small mnemonic for flexibility.
usage_notes: 'luwi kelu' means become flexible or bend. For a garment, 'luwi' describes the material, while 'ralu' (free) describes being loose or unconstrained.

semantic_domains.physical: bending or changing shape without damage
